2015-11-26.abstract: As Crimean Tatar activists and Ukrainian nationalists prevented repair crews from getting to downed electricity pylons, Kiev closed its airspace to Russian planes and Moscow cut off gas supplies to Ukraine.

2015-11-26.title: After Paris Attacks, Ties That Bind Patrons at a Cafe Also Burn
2015-11-26.abstract: In a city where corner bistros are havens for neighbors and friends, the terrorist attacks in Paris hurt families related by blood and banter.

2015-11-26.title: Court in China Reduces Prison Sentence for Gao Yu, Prominent Journalist
2015-11-26.abstract: Ms. Gao, 71, who was convicted of leaking a document that laid out plans for a campaign against Western ideas, will now serve five years rather than seven.

2015-11-26.title: North and South Korea to Hold High-Level Talks Next Month
2015-11-26.abstract: The talks, to be led by a vice minister from each side, are intended to ease tensions and improve relations on the divided Korean Peninsula.

2015-11-26.title: From Indonesia, a Muslim Challenge to the Ideology of the Islamic State
2015-11-26.abstract: The campaign comes at a time when Islam is at war with itself over central theological questions of how the faith is defined.

2015-11-27.title: El Salvador: Priest Faces Abuse Inquiry
2015-11-27.abstract: A high-ranking priest was suspended while the church investigated allegations that he had sexually abused a girl over an eight-year period.

2015-11-27.title: Cellphone Recording Leads to Arrest of Brazilian Senator on Obstruction Charges
2015-11-27.abstract: Delcídio do Amaral, an ally of President Dilma Rousseff, was charged with impeding an investigation into Brazil’s state-owned oil company.

2015-11-27.title: Gunmen Strike Shiite Mosque in Bangladesh
2015-11-27.abstract: Gunmen opened fire during evening prayers at the mosque, killing one person and wounding three, in the second recent attack on Bangladesh’s tiny Shiite population.

2015-11-27.title: Paris Slowly Coming to Terms With a New Vulnerability
2015-11-27.abstract: The city wants to get past the lost tourism and uncertainties that followed the Nov. 13 attacks. But as candles burn low and bouquets wilt, a certain shock lingers.

2015-11-27.title: Chinese Rights Advocate Known as Guo Feixiong Convicted of Unexpected New Charge
2015-11-27.abstract: The addition of new charge, “picking quarrels and provoking trouble,” means that he will spend an additional two years in prison, according to his lawyers.

2015-11-28.title: Cubans Protest Stricter Rules for Traveling to Ecuador
2015-11-28.abstract: Cubans crowded outside an Ecuadorean embassy, worried about a new visa requirement to enter Ecuador, a vital step to reach the United States.

2015-11-28.title: Paris Climate Talks Avoid Scientists’ Idea of ‘Carbon Budget’
2015-11-28.abstract: Nations have pointedly declined to take up a recommendation from scientists, made several years ago, that they set a cap on total greenhouse gases.

2015-11-28.title: Tibetans Fight to Salvage Fading Culture in China
2015-11-28.abstract: New measures scaling back the teaching of minority languages like Tibetan have threatened the survival of ethnic identities and traditions in China’s vast west.

2015-11-28.title: Tashi Wangchuk: A Tibetan’s Journey for Justice
2015-11-28.abstract: Worried about the erosion of Tibetan culture and language, one man takes his concerns to Beijing, hoping media coverage and the courts can reverse what he sees as a systematic eradication.

2015-11-28.title: 一名藏人的追求正义之路
2015-11-28.abstract: 带着对本族语言和文化即将消亡的担忧，一名男子来到北京，希望媒体报道和法院能够改变这种在他看来是系统性灭绝的现状。

2015-11-29.title: ISIS’ Grip on Libyan City Gives It a Fallback Option
2015-11-29.abstract: The Mediterranean port city of Surt has become an actively managed colony of the Islamic State, which has come under growing military and economic pressure in Syria and Iraq.

2015-11-29.title: 1890: Germany’s Gift to the World
2015-11-29.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: In Germany, there is hope for a cure to tuberculosis, in 1890.

2015-11-29.title: 1940: Rome Declares Counterattack Has Halted Foe
2015-11-29.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: Italy says it has brought the Greek advance to a standstill in 1940.

2015-11-29.title: E.U. Offers Turkey 3 Billion Euros to Stem Migrant Flow
2015-11-29.abstract: European Union leaders met with Turkey’s prime minister on Sunday, promising aid and other inducements in exchange for Turkish action to clamp down on asylum seekers.

2015-11-29.title: Chinese Report on Climate Change Depicts Somber Scenarios
2015-11-29.abstract: An assessment from state-appointed experts was released just before negotiations in Paris for a new international agreement to lower greenhouse-gas pollution.

2015-11-30.title: In Africa, Pope Francis Makes His First Visit to a War Zone
2015-11-30.abstract: Pope Francis landed in Bangui, capital of the divided and destitute Central African Republic, in a bid to support efforts at peace and reconciliation.

2015-11-30.title: France Uses Sweeping Powers to Curb Climate Protests, but Clashes Erupt
2015-11-30.abstract: Despite efforts to limit any disruption to a two-week global climate conference in Paris, 174 people were taken into custody when demonstrators fought with the police.

2015-11-30.title: Predatory Islamic State Wrings Money From Those It Rules
2015-11-30.abstract: The earnings from the militants’ violent collection practices total tens of millions of dollars a month, approaching $1 billion a year, according to some estimates.

2015-11-30.title: Hope for Nefertiti’s Tomb, and Egypt’s Economy
2015-11-30.abstract: The prospect of discovering the long-sought tomb of Queen Nefertiti would be momentous. It would also help Egypt’s tourism industry, which has plummeted in recent years because of political unrest.

2015-11-30.title: Discord Between Turkey and Russia Is Fueled by Leaders’ Similarities
2015-11-30.abstract: Not long ago, Recep Tayyip Erdogan, the president of Turkey, earned about the warmest accolade possible from President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia. That was then.
